displayMetadata.name: KEY INFORMATION
displayMetadata.value:
• 1. Preheat oven to 160°C (fan forced) or 180°C (conventional oven).
• 2. Spray the tray with vegetable oil (if you have patty cases, place them in the tray).
• 3. Add 2 eggs (or egg replacer), 60 g margarine (room temperature) and water to small mixing bowl then add the red velvet Cupcake mix.
• 4. Using an electric mixer, mix on slow speed for 1 minute. Scrape down sides of bowl.

displayMetadata.name: STORAGE INFORMATION
displayMetadata.value: Store at room temperature. As a precaution, however, it is recommended to keep certain products away from heat and humidity (see recommendations on the package).

product.description: Who needs a special occasion to bake a pan of fudgy brownies or a double batch of chocolate chip cookies. Desserts are one of Betty Crocker’s claims to fame, it’s not hard to see why.

• This product contains: Wheat, soya products
• Manufactured on equipment that also processes milk products and egg products
metaData.Key Information: A tasty treat that is so easy to make.
metaData.Preparation:
• How To Prepare:
1. Put PrimaMix Doughnut Mix in a mixer, add water and mix at low speed (2 minutes) or at medium speed (9 minutes)
2. Cover the dough in the mixer with a moist cloth and let it stand for about 25 minutes at room temperature
3. Repeat the rolling and folding
4. Cover dough with a moist cloth and let it rest for 5 minutes
5. Roll the dough to even thickness of 10 mm and dust with flour to prevent sticking
6. Cut rings of doughnuts and roll the remaining dough into a sheet and cut again
7. Arrange doughnuts on a greased flat surface, cover with a moist cloth and let it rise for about 1 hour at room temperature
8. Heat up oil in deep fryer to 180°C (medium heat) and test the oil with a remaining piece of dough
9. Fry doughnuts till golden brown (1 - 2 minutes on each side)
metaData.Storage Information: Store in a cool, dry place

Contains acidity regulator as permitted food conditioner. Contains permitted flavouring substance.

metaData.Key Information: Weekend mornings are for pancakes! Perfect your Saturday mornings by getting your pancakes ready faster with the Betty Crocker Pancake Mix!Quit worrying about the hassle which comes with making pancakes. Say no more to doing the dirty dishes or creating a mess in your kitchen! Scoop some ready-made pancake mix into a bowl, crack an egg, add some milk, and you’re ready to start cooking!
metaData.Nutritional Data:
Attributes Per Serving (19.5g)

Total Fat 1.1g
Saturated Fat 0.4g

Carbohydrate 13.6g
Sugars 2.5g
Dietary Fibre 0.4g
Protein 1.7g
Salt 0.4g
Sodium 169mg
metaData.Preparation:
Directions:For 8 to 10 pancakes:
• 1 cup Betty Crocker pancake mix
• 2?3 cup (160ml) milk
•1 egg 1 tbsp oil1. Heat the pan on medium heat until drop of water sizzle and evaporate quickly. Grease the pan if necessary.2. Add all ingredients to a bowl and stir with a fork until well combined.
For thinner pancakes, add more milk and for thicker pancakes, add more pancake mix.3. For each pancake, pour 1?4 cup batter onto the hot pan.4. Cook till bubbles break on the surface and edges begin to dry.5. Turn the pancake and cook the other side for 1 minute or till golden brown.Others: Dress up your pancakes! Add blueberries, chocolate chips, or sliced bananas into the batter just before flipping.
metaData.Storage Information:
• Refrigerate upon openingHow to freeze & reheat pancakes:
1. Let the pancakes cool to room temperature
2. Layer between sheets of parchment paper and seal the pancakes in a zipper bag.
3. To reheat, spread a few frozen pancakes on a plate in a single layer
4. Heat it up in the microwave for about 30 to 45 seconds
5. Pop them in the toaster

metaData.Key Information:
• Preheat oven to 160°C (fan forced) or 180°C (conventional oven).
• 2. Spray the Cupcake tray with vegetable oil or place patty cases in the Cupcake tray.
• 3. Add 2 eggs (or egg replacer), 60 g margarine (room temperature) and 160 ML water to a mixing bowl then add the vanilla Cupcake mix.
• 4. Using an electric mixer, mix on slow speed for 1 minute, scrape down side of bowl. Scrape down sides of bowl. Mix on medium speed for 1 minute.
• 5. Pour batter into tray and bake for approx. 20-25 minutes*. *Cupcakes are baked if they bounce back when gently pressed at the peak.
• 6. Remove Cupcakes from oven and place them onto wire rack to cool.
metaData.SAP Product Name: Yes You Can Cupcake Mix - Vanilla
metaData.Storage Information: Store at room temperature. As a precaution, however, it is recommended to keep certain products away from heat and humidity (see recommendations on the package).

metaData.Key Information:
• 1. Preheat oven to 160 °C (fan forced) or 180 °C (conventional oven).
• 2. Grease tin or spray with vegetable oil.
• 3. Add 2 eggs, 100 ML oil, and 150 ML water into mixing bowl then add cake mix.
• 4. Fold mixture with a wooden spoon. Scrape down sides of bowl.
• 5. Pour batter into cake in and bake for approx. 45-50 minutes. Cake is baked if it bounces back when pressed at the peak of the cake.
• 6. Remove from oven. Leave in tin for 10 minutes to cool. Cooking times indicated should be used as a guide only, as appliances vary.
